items.front: When aircraft choose to take delay on the ground before departure, what is the normal sequencing basis?
items.back: The order in which the requests for clearance were originally made, if practicable.

items.front: What qualifier applies to preserving the original request order in ground-delay sequencing?
items.back: If practicable.

items.front: Does paragraph 4-3-6 address delay taken on the ground or in holding after departure?
items.back: On the ground before departure.

items.front: What should not normally displace the first requester in ground-delay sequencing?
items.back: A later requester simply being faster or more convenient to launch.

items.front: Who must be informed of anticipated departure delays?
items.back: Approach control facilities and/or towers.

items.front: What kind of delays are covered in paragraph 4-3-7?
items.back: Anticipated departure delays.

items.front: Is a tower one of the explicit coordination targets in paragraph 4-3-7?
items.back: Yes.

items.front: What is the basic controller action in paragraph 4-3-7?
items.back: Inform the affected approach control facilities and/or towers.

items.front: What is the baseline predeparture coordination trigger in paragraph 4-3-8?
items.back: The departure point is less than 15 minutes flying time from the transferring facility's boundary.

items.front: When automatic transfer of data between automated systems will occur, to what threshold may the flying-time requirement be reduced?
items.back: 5 minutes.

items.front: What can replace the flying-time trigger when automatic data transfer will occur and both facilities agree?
items.back: A mileage-from-boundary parameter.

items.front: What kind of agreements are encouraged when facilities need earlier coordination?
items.back: Agreements requiring additional time.

items.front: If agreements establish mandatory radar handoff procedures, when must coordination be effected?
items.back: In a timely manner prior to transfer of control.

items.front: Does mandatory radar handoff automatically eliminate coordination?
items.back: No.

items.front: After any necessary approval, what may ATC authorize for an aircraft that filed IFR but requests to depart VFR?
items.back: A VFR departure with instructions on the proper frequency and, if appropriate, when or where to contact for the IFR clearance.

items.front: What phrase begins the VFR release of an IFR departure?
items.back: VFR departure authorized.

items.front: What should be suggested if the facility responsible for the IFR clearance cannot issue it?
items.back: That the delay be taken on the ground.

items.front: If the pilot insists on departing VFR and obtaining the IFR clearance in the air, who must be informed?
items.back: The facility or sector holding the flight plan.

items.front: If possible, what additional detail should be given to the facility or sector holding the flight plan when the pilot insists on a VFR departure?
items.back: The VFR departure time.

items.front: Which facilities are named in paragraph 4-3-9 as places where this VFR release request may be handled?
items.back: A terminal facility, FSS, ARTCC Flight Data Unit, or air/ground communications station.

items.front: Does paragraph 4-3-9 apply to an aircraft that has filed an IFR flight plan?
items.back: Yes.

items.front: Unless alternate procedures apply, where must terminal departure times be forwarded?
items.back: To the facility from which the clearance was received and to the terminal departure controller when that position is involved.

items.front: What is one explicit exception to the normal forwarding requirement in paragraph 4-3-10?
items.back: Automatic departure messages are being transmitted between automated facilities.

items.front: What does a flashing DM in the data block signify?
items.back: Unsuccessful transmission of a departure message.

items.front: What LOA procedures are named as alternatives for equivalent handling in the note to paragraph 4-3-10?
items.back: Assumed departure times or mandatory radar handoff procedures.

items.front: When must the terminal departure controller receive the forwarded departure time?
items.back: When that position is involved in the departure sequence.
